raw_prompt: The Boss asks: "Compute WACC. Cost of equity is 10%, weight of equity is 60%, weight of debt is 40%, cost of debt is 5%, and tax rate is 20%."
raw_response:
METHOD: Weighted Average Cost of Capital (WACC)
INPUTS:
  Re  = 10%    [source: Boss-supplied, VERIFIED]
  We  = 60%    [source: Boss-supplied, VERIFIED]
  Wd  = 40%    [source: Boss-supplied, VERIFIED]
  Rd  = 5%     [source: Boss-supplied, VERIFIED]
  Tc  = 20%    [source: Boss-supplied, VERIFIED]
FORMULA: WACC = We*Re + Wd*Rd*(1 - Tc)
SUBSTITUTION: WACC = 0.60*0.10 + 0.40*0.05*(1 - 0.20) = 0.060 + 0.016 = 0.076
RESULT: WACC = 7.6%  [unit: percent per year]
SENSITIVITY: +/-1pt Rd -> +/-0.32pt; +/-5pt Tc -> +/-0.08pt
ASSUMPTIONS: target capital-structure weights, constant tax rate.
evidence_quotes: No DATA UNAVAILABLE for supplied data
